# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// import the required packages 
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
  
export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) : Promise<NextResponse>{     // declare a new exported method with parameter of type ``Promise (which returns an instance which can be wrapped by the response to send as HTTP Responses. “async” and it accepts two parameters that correspond directly onto those in your next request object, NextRequest)
   try {    // wrap our code inside a 'try' block - error handling will happen here so if there are any errors they can be caught by the catch statement below       const authCookie = req.cookies['auth-token']  ;      get cookies from cookie named `authToken` on request object
   ...     // write your code inside this function body based upon requirements (authentication logic) and check authentication status; if authenticated, return response with access or proceed to next middleware/handler else reject it. If unauthenticated: then redirect user to login page using NextResponse inverts the http request context
   } catch(e){    // write code inside 'catch' block where you can handle any error by logging them and returning a generic response;  if there is an unhandled promise rejection or async/await errors, this will happen here.     console .error('Error in AuthMiddleware: ', e)
   return NextResponse.redirect('/login') // redirect user to login page on error case scenario and send 'unauthorized' response  }      if (!authCookie){    // handle unauthenticated users ie not logged-in by returning a HTTP Redirection; this will happen only when no auth token is found in the cookie.     return NextResponse .redirect ('/login')
   ...}// write your code inside these braces and fill it with logic based upon requirements (authentication)  } catch(e){ console -error('Error: ', e)} // Handle unhandled promise rejection or async await errors; If there's an error here, this will be caught by the 'catch block'.   
   return res      ;     Incase no Promise Rejections in either if/else blocks above are encountered then it should come to finally. This gets executed after all try-except clauses have been reached ie., even upon catching exceptions or encountering errors at any point  console .log('Finally runs before leaving this block',);
}// closes the function
